north_star_verdict.headline: Pre-discovery Qualified deal that has never held a buyer call, single-threaded through Cedric Elmaleth who is on paternity leave until mid-July. RFP for Sourcing, CLM and Tailspend expected September/October, but no discovery, no Economic Buyer mapped, and no buyer inbound since qualification.
north_star_verdict.math: Qualified 2026-04-30, so 67 days in stage as of today 2026-07-06 and 100 percent of the deal's life spent in Qualified with no progression. Close date 2026-11-28 is 145 days out. Last touch 2026-06-15 was Pierre's outbound email that drew only Cedric's paternity-leave auto-reply (back mid-July per the 25 Jun Next Step), so there is no genuine two-way buyer engagement on record. The stated forcing event, an RFP for Sourcing, CLM and Tailspend, is 10 to 14 weeks out (Sept/Oct). Pace is behind: a Qualified deal with zero discovery calls and no Economic Buyer 145 days from a November close is stalled on the buyer side, not off-track-cold, because a known RFP event and a legitimate leave explain the pause.

metrics.narrative: The business problem is documented but never quantified. Opella is the consumer-healthcare spin-off of Sanofi (carve-out) now running inherited legacy SAP and Coupa systems that do not fit its new independent structure, and it lacks dedicated Sourcing and Tail Spend tooling (SF Customer_Business_Problem__c). No cost-savings target, headcount reduction, cycle-time or error-rate figure has been captured because zero discovery calls have occurred: the pain is qualitative and Zycus-inferred, not buyer-quantified. Quantifying the value case with Cedric Elmaleth or Lyvio Frimat in a first discovery session is the immediate metrics gap to close.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified and none can be inferred, because there has been no buyer conversation to reveal who controls budget. All five OpportunityContactRole contacts are Evaluator (Cedric Elmaleth, Marie-Annick Luro, Emeline Turmeau) or Influencer (Lyvio Frimat, Claire Muret Grimbert); no CFO or C-level budget holder is mapped. The strongest budget-authority candidate is Lyvio Frimat, VP and Global Head of Procurement Centre of Excellence, Strategy and Transformation, whose mandate over an S2C transformation likely carries or shapes the budget, but he has not been contacted and his authority is unconfirmed. Mapping the Economic Buyer, starting by engaging Lyvio Frimat, is a ranked move before any commercial step.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (5 contacts, none EB/Executive Sponsor)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are not yet known because no discovery, demo or workshop has taken place and no Business_Requirements field is populated. What the evidence does show is the shape of the coming evaluation: the Next Step (2026-06-15) states an RFP is expected September/October 2026 covering Sourcing, CLM and Tailspend, so the criteria will be set formally through that RFP rather than through prior use-case sessions. The move is to run pre-RFP discovery so Zycus can influence the requirements (especially the AI/Merlin and tail-spend criteria) before the document is issued.
sources: Next_Step__c 2026-06-15 (RFP for Sourcing, CLM, Tailspend, Sept/Oct)

decision_process.narrative: The process the buyer is running is a formal RFP for Sourcing, CLM and Tailspend expected September/October 2026 (Next_Step__c 2026-06-15), preceded by a discovery phase that has not yet started. The call sequence cannot be read as a process map because no calls exist; the only process signal is the RFP timeline itself and the fact that Cedric Elmaleth (Senior Director of Procurement) is the current entry point. The approval chain and number of approvals are undocumented (X10a/approval fields silent), so the decision process is only partially visible and must be surfaced in discovery.

paper_process.narrative: Paper process is unknown and appropriately so at Qualified, pre-discovery. No MSA, SOW, legal, InfoSec or procurement-channel detail is recorded, and Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null so there is no contract-expiry forcing function. The one paper-relevant signal is that CLM is part of the coming RFP scope and a CLM vendor may already be selected (prior record), which could affect how iContract is contracted if there is overlap. Visibility will come only once discovery starts and the RFP defines the procurement and contracting path.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is identified at a qualitative level but not buyer-validated. Post-carve-out from Sanofi, Opella runs inherited legacy SAP and Coupa systems misaligned with its new independent operating model and has no dedicated Sourcing or Tail Spend solution (SF Customer_Business_Problem__c). This is Zycus-inferred pain, with no buyer quote to confirm it because no discovery call has happened. The likely pain owner is senior procurement leadership, Lyvio Frimat (VP Procurement CoE, Strategy and Transformation) or Marie-Annick Luro (Global Head Procurement Effectiveness Excellence), neither of whom has been engaged. Validating and owning this pain with a named senior buyer is the immediate move.

champion.narrative: There is no working champion, only a weakly-named contact. Cedric Elmaleth (Senior Director of Procurement) is named as the champion in prior record (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c), but his OpportunityContactRole is Evaluator, not Champion, and every touch has been one-way from Zycus: Pierre's 2026-06-15 email drew only a paternity-leave auto-reply, and Cedric is out until mid-July with no advocacy, no internal introductions and no agenda-driving on record. Claire Muret Grimbert is the primary contact but is an Influencer in Marketing/Sales procurement, adjacent to the S2C scope. Building a real champion, re-engaging Cedric on his return and testing Lyvio Frimat as a stronger, more senior candidate, is the top continuous priority.

competition.narrative: There is no active named vendor bake-off; the real contest is inherited-stack inertia and do-nothing. Opella runs Sanofi-legacy SAP and Coupa, and prior record notes a CLM vendor has already been selected, which narrows the iContract opening (SF Why_not_Zycus__c). The risk is that SAP or Coupa, or an integrated extension such as SAP Ariba for sourcing, gets positioned as the low-risk continuation of the existing stack, and that the newly-independent Opella simply keeps managing sourcing and contracts on legacy tooling rather than committing to change. Zycus's wedge is its AI footprint (ANA for tail spend, the Merlin suite for sourcing automation and decision intelligence) that legacy SAP/Coupa lack, but this has never been tested with the buyer (zero calls).
sources: SF Why_not_Zycus__c (SAP + Coupa ecosystem, CLM vendor selected)
SF Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c (AI suite, ANA tail spend)

deal_movement.summary: Since qualification on 2026-04-30 the deal has produced no buyer-side movement: the only activity is Zycus-initiated outreach. The last substantive event is Pierre's 2026-06-15 email to Cedric, which returned a paternity-leave auto-reply, and the 2026-06-25 Next Step update noting Cedric is out until mid-July. Field history shows only null-to-null Next Step edits, meaning no real progression is being logged.

competitive_position.summary: This is not an active vendor fight; the strongest current threat is do-nothing / inherited-stack inertia. Opella runs Sanofi-legacy SAP and Coupa (2026-04-28 context) and, per prior record, has already selected a CLM vendor, which trims the iContract opening. No rival has been named on any buyer call because no call has happened, so all threat levels are dormant/low pending discovery. The win path is to prove that staying on the inherited Sanofi-era stack perpetuates the misfit for an independent Opella, and to land the AI/tail-spend story before the Sept/Oct RFP frames the field.

competitors.quote: Opella is operating on inherited legacy systems from Sanofi (SAP plus Coupa) which may not align with their new independent structure.
competitors.how_we_win: Frame inherited-stack drift as the risk for a newly independent Opella; lead with ANA tail-spend and Merlin sourcing automation legacy SAP/Coupa cannot match, before the RFP.

competitors.how_we_win: Position S2C transformation depth and AI decisioning against a generic ERP-extension sourcing path.

competitors.name: Coupa (inherited incumbent)
competitors.quote: Existing ecosystem with SAP and Coupa may favor incumbents or integrated extensions.
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Merlin autonomous sourcing and ANA tail-spend coverage against Coupa's footprint in the inherited stack.

competitors.name: Already-selected CLM vendor
competitors.quote: CLM vendor already selected, reducing iContract footprint.
competitors.how_we_win: Concede CLM if lost and anchor on iSource + ANA + Merlin for Sourcing and Tailspend, where no vendor is yet chosen; keep iContract as a Phase 2 consolidation play.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage, single-threaded, pre-discovery deal with a known RFP event on the horizon. The win is set now by getting into discovery before the Sept/Oct RFP and by broadening beyond one on-leave contact.
flags: Get into pre-RFP discovery before Sept/Oct: shaping the Sourcing/Tailspend requirements now is the single highest-impact lever to avoid a spec written around the incumbent stack.
Break single-thread risk: engage Lyvio Frimat (VP Procurement CoE, Strategy and Transformation) as a more senior entry point while Cedric is on leave until mid-July.

Lead with the AI wedge: put ANA tail-spend and Merlin sourcing automation in front of the buyer, since three AI SKUs are the only differentiation versus the inherited SAP/Coupa stack and it has never been tested.

champion_strength.summary: Champion is weak and passive. Cedric Elmaleth is the named champion (prior X10b) but holds an Evaluator role, has never advocated, and every touch has been one-way from Zycus; his 2026-06-15 reply was an automated paternity-leave notice and he is out until mid-July. No internal introductions, shared docs, or agenda-driving exist. Lyvio Frimat is an untested but more senior candidate. Champion-building is the top continuous priority.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus has positioned three AI SKUs (ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake) alongside iContract and iSource, a strong AI footprint for an S2C Pharma deal, but the AI narrative has never been put to the buyer (zero calls), so it is under-positioned in the buyer's mind despite being the core differentiator versus the inherited SAP/Coupa stack.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 5.0 / Moderate: rich AI product scope (three AI SKUs) but no engagement evidence to confirm AI as the buying driver. The AI appetite is a Zycus positioning bet, not yet a buyer-expressed need, so treat it as curious rather than hungry until a discovery call tests it.
